projectMetadata.clientOrganization: The Auto Lot (formerly Alvarez Auto Sales)

executiveSummary: MH Construction served as general contractor for The Auto Lot at 3809 Plaza Way in Kennewick, Washington, supporting the owner's transition from Alvarez Auto Sales to a larger public-facing dealership location. Public reporting cited a completed 4,200-square-foot facility with showroom, storefront glazing, paved inventory circulation, and site improvements.
clientBackground: The Auto Lot is a family-owned used-vehicle dealership in Kennewick that transitioned from Alvarez Auto Sales to an expanded Plaza Way location to support greater inventory and customer flow.
challenge: Expand from a constrained prior site to a higher-capacity dealership environment with visible branding, customer circulation, accessible routes, and coordinated building/site delivery.
scopeOfWork: General contracting for a new commercial automotive dealership facility
Coordination of building and site work for showroom, offices, inventory circulation, and parking
Delivery of exterior envelope finishes, storefront systems, and dealership signage interfaces
Delivery of interior showroom finishes, display zones, and customer-facing elements
Coordination with project design documents and municipal review requirements

timelineAndMilestones.name: Design record
timelineAndMilestones.note: Public architectural plan set identified the Plaza Way dealership project.

timelineAndMilestones.name: Construction underway
timelineAndMilestones.note: Local coverage reported active construction and identified MH Construction as general contractor.

timelineAndMilestones.name: Completion reported
timelineAndMilestones.note: Local business reporting cited completion and grand-opening announcement.

timelineAndMilestones.name: Grand opening
timelineAndMilestones.note: Publicly announced opening date for The Auto Lot.

timelineAndMilestones.name: Closeout media capture
timelineAndMilestones.note: Exterior and interior media captured to document completed facility conditions.

executionDetails: MH Construction's verified role was general contractor with Baker Architecture identified publicly as project designer. Verified media documents storefront, lot circulation, site improvements, and customer-facing showroom environment.
outcomesAndImpact: Enabled transition to a larger, more visible commercial site
Expanded inventory and customer circulation capacity
Established a consistent customer experience across lot, entry, and showroom
Supported owner brand transition to The Auto Lot

safetyAndComplianceNotes.codeBasis: 2018 IBC with Washington amendments and related Washington code requirements
